_instructions.purpose: Lightweight session loader. Fetch this file at session start and acknowledge it is loaded. The index below is the sole source of domain/subtopic information — never fetch knowledge_base.json just to show an index.
how_to_load: 1. At session start: fetch session_loader.json via GitHub MCP. Acknowledge it is loaded. Do NOT fetch knowledge_base.json yet.

2. When the user makes a request: read the index in THIS file to identify the most relevant domain(s) and subtopic(s). Commit to a specific recommendation — do not list all options and ask the user to choose. Only present alternatives if the request is genuinely ambiguous across two equally plausible chunks.
3. State your recommended chunk(s) with a brief rationale and ask for confirmation to load. Example: 'This looks like concepts.frameworks — shall I load that?'
4. NEVER fetch knowledge_base.json to answer an index/listing question. The index here is sufficient. Only fetch knowledge_base.json after the user confirms a specific chunk to load.
5. After confirmation: fetch knowledge_base.json via GitHub MCP and extract ONLY the confirmed chunk(s) by key path.
6. After reading a chunk: summarise what was loaded and proceed with the task.
7. Never speculatively read additional chunks unless the user explicitly requests it.

8. RETRIEVAL DISCIPLINE: The index in this file is complete for navigation purposes. Fetching the full knowledge base to inspect subtopics is always wrong unless a chunk has been confirmed for loading.
_instructions.note: The index below is the authoritative domain/subtopic map. Sub-topic content lives only in knowledge_base.json.

meta._summary: Project-level metadata: author, dates, versioning, global tags.

research._summary: Core research areas, sources, and findings.

concepts._summary: Key terms, definitions, and conceptual frameworks.

entities._summary: Named entities: people, organizations, locations, artifacts.

timeline._summary: Chronological events and milestones relevant to the project.

notes._summary: Working notes, drafts, and session logs.
